On the basis of these observations, we got the confirmation results for the targeted
drug  delivery  via  i.n.  to  brain  [17].  The  entrapment  of  active  constituent  (MNG)
inside the nanoformulation core which is very essential of release of drug and their
delivery  at  specific  time  from  their  colloidal-system  to  provide  us  an  appropriate
physiological response. Hence, the alteration of drug-release as modified the charac-
teristics of physicochemical medium. The Mangiferin was entrapped inside the core
of P 80 MNG PLGA NPs which was delivered through i.n. route to CNS at a pH:7.4
that  is  almost  equal  to  the  CNS  pH  of  cells.  The  nanoformulations  containing  the
surface charge and their size are measured as a key point for Enhanced permeabil-
ity  and  retention,  i.e.,  EPR  effect.  The  size  of  particle  gives  a  maximum  duration
of staying time in the nasal mucosa and their charge of particles provides the easi-
ness to target for the brain followed by their release of drug. The size of particle is
the main factor to provide the route to maximum staying time in the nasal mucosa
